name: Prapratno ferry port (→ Mljet)
description: Car-ferry harbour in a sandy cove ~3 km west of Ston on Pelješac. Jadrolinija line 832 to Sobra (Mljet), ~45 min crossing. Our main island gateway.
communityNotes: Official Jadrolinija line 832 is the source to check before driving to the port. Cars are first-come, first-served — a date ticket does NOT guarantee a deck slot. Travelers report needing to be in the vehicle queue 60–120 min before departure in peak summer, more on weekends. June (29 May–2 Jul block) = 5 sailings/day. The cove itself has 'one of the most beautiful sandy beaches in the Adriatic'.
cost: 4 adults + car ≈ €49.90 each way (adult €6.10, car €25.50, 2026 high season)
bestTime: Day 4 (Fri Jun 19) — Friday = lighter queue than Saturday. Target 07:00–08:00 sailing for maximum island time; queue 60+ min early. 13:00 is the last comfortable outbound; 17:00 plan-B only.

name: Sobra ferry port (Mljet)
description: Mljet's main car-ferry port (north coast), the Mljet end of the Prapratno line. ~half-hour drive from the Mljet NP lakes / Montokuc viewpoint at the west end.
communityNotes: Same first-come-first-served rule as Prapratno. Rough weather can delay Sobra departures ~60 min. June departures to Prapratno: 06:00, 09:00, 12:00, 16:00, 19:30.
bestTime: Day 5 (Jun 20) — June sailings from Sobra: 06:00, 09:00, 12:00, 16:00, 19:30. To reach Kravica before crowds arrive: catch the 06:00 — depart camp by 05:30, arrive Prapratno ~06:45, drive 2h → Kravica 08:45. Otherwise 09:00 sailing lands at Kravica ~11:30 (still swimmable, busier). First-come; same queue rules as Prapratno.

name: Ploče ferry port (→ Trpanj)
description: Mainland port for the Jadrolinija line 633 car ferry to Trpanj on Pelješac, ~60 min. An old alternative to the Neum corridor — now largely superseded by the toll-free Pelješac Bridge.
communityNotes: Since the Pelješac Bridge opened (2022) this line's traffic dropped ~95%, so no more boarding chaos — but in June only 3 sailings/day (10:30, 14:15, 19:30), so it's schedule-rigid. For reaching Dubrovnik the bridge is faster and crosses no border; use the ferry only if Trpanj/Pelješac is the actual destination.
cost: 4 adults + car ≈ €36 each way (adult €4.50, car €18.00, 2026 low season)

name: Trpanj ferry port (Pelješac)
description: Pelješac-side port of the Ploče–Trpanj car ferry (line 633). Small harbour town on the peninsula's north coast.
communityNotes: June departures to Ploče: 06:45, 12:00, 17:15 (3/day). First-come for vehicles, but rarely busy post-bridge.

name: Orebić ferry port (→ Korčula)
description: Pelješac port for the Jadrolinija line 634 car ferry to Dominče (Korčula), ~15–20 min. Frequent short shuttle (~05:30–22:30, dozens/day in summer).
communityNotes: Cheap and frequent enough that car queues are rarely a real problem, though still first-come. Buy the car ticket online for peak days to avoid being bumped when full. Note: the separate Orebić–Korčula-town boat (line 614, G&V Line Iadera) is foot-passengers only.
cost: 4 adults + car ≈ €14–15 each way (adult €2.50, car €4.40, 2026 high season)

name: Debeli Brijeg / Karasovići border (ME↔HR)
description: Main Montenegro↔Croatia coastal crossing on the Kotor/Herceg Novi → Dubrovnik route. The trip's biggest queue risk on the Jun 27/28 return.
communityNotes: Summer waits 1–4 h, up to 4–5 h on peak days (Fri/Sun worst); worst window 10:00–14:00 with Dubrovnik day-trippers. Cross before 08:00 or after 21:00. Back-door alternative: Vitaljina/Kobila (Konfin) via Molunat — cars only, ~1 h max even on peak weekends. Check live: kamere.mup.gov.me, nakordoni.eu, borderalarm.com. Note EES (live since 10 Apr 2026) adds biometric checks for non-EU travelers.
bestTime: Day 11 (Jun 26) — DO NOT USE for ME→HR. EES biometric queues 1–4h. Use VITALJINA/Karasovići (15 km south of Kotor, via Herceg Novi) instead — 15–45 min wait. Only fall back here before 08:00 or after 21:00 if Vitaljina is closed.
